items.title: Interactive Phase Dependency Graph
items.description: Visual TUI or HTML rendering of phase dependencies, showing which phases are blocked, ready, in-flight, or complete. Users can't easily see the critical path when planning multi-phase milestones — this gives them a Gantt-like view directly in the terminal or browser, revealing parallelization opportunities and bottlenecks at a glance.

items.title: Research Citation Backlink Tracker
items.description: Automatically tracks which phases, plans, and SUMMARY.md files reference each paper in PAPERS.md. When a user asks 'where did we actually use this paper?', they currently have to grep manually. This adds a reverse index so every paper shows its downstream usage, helping prune stale research and understand which papers drove real implementation decisions.

items.title: Phase Risk Assessment Agent
items.description: Before executing a phase, analyze the plan files for risk signals: vague success criteria, missing baselines, overly large scope, unclear dependencies, or no fallback strategy. Surfaces a risk score with actionable remediation suggestions. Prevents teams from executing poorly-scoped phases that will fail verification and require expensive rework.

items.title: Cross-Project Knowledge Import
items.description: Import LANDSCAPE.md, PAPERS.md, or KNOWHOW.md from another GRD project into the current one. Researchers often start new projects in adjacent domains where prior survey work is highly relevant. Instead of re-running expensive surveys, they can pull vetted knowledge from a sibling project and annotate what transfers vs. what needs updating.

items.title: Evaluation Regression Alerts
items.description: After each eval-report run, automatically compare metrics against the stored baseline and emit a prominent warning (or block completion) if any key metric regressed beyond a configurable threshold. Users currently have to manually diff eval results — this makes regressions impossible to miss and catches them before they compound across phases.

items.title: Plan Template Library
items.description: A curated set of PLAN.md templates for common R&D patterns: ablation study, dataset collection, model fine-tuning, API integration, benchmark reproduction. When running /grd:plan-phase, the planner can select or suggest a template as a starting scaffold. Reduces the blank-page problem and ensures common verification steps aren't forgotten.

items.title: Natural Language Progress Query
items.description: Ask questions like 'what did we ship last week?', 'which phases are over budget?', 'what blockers are unresolved?' and get structured answers synthesized from STATE.md, SUMMARY.md files, and roadmap. Currently users must manually read multiple files. This makes project status instantly accessible without context-loading overhead.

items.title: Experiment Notebook Export
items.description: Export a phase's research + plans + summaries + eval results as a self-contained Jupyter notebook or Markdown report suitable for sharing with stakeholders or publishing as a blog post. Bridges the gap between internal R&D artifacts and external communication — currently researchers must manually reformat everything when presenting results.

items.title: Automated Literature Gap Detection
items.description: After surveying a topic, automatically compare the methods in LANDSCAPE.md against the implementation in plans/code to identify research areas that were surveyed but never attempted. Surfaces 'we read about X but never tried it' gaps, helping teams decide whether to revisit promising approaches before closing a milestone.

items.title: Phase Time Budget Tracking
items.description: Track actual wall-clock time per phase against the declared Duration field, and surface variance trends across the project. Teams consistently underestimate phase duration — this gives historical data to calibrate future estimates and flags phases that are running 2x+ over budget before they derail the milestone timeline.

items.title: Multi-Repo GRD Federation
items.description: A parent project that aggregates STATE.md and progress from multiple child GRD projects (e.g., separate repos for data pipeline, model training, and serving). Research platforms often span multiple repos — this gives a unified dashboard showing cross-repo phase status, blockers, and eval metrics in one view without requiring a monorepo.

items.title: Requirement Coverage Heatmap
items.description: Visualize which requirements from REQUIREMENTS.md are covered by which phases and plans, with color coding for coverage density. Uncovers requirements that have zero plan coverage (at risk of being forgotten) and requirements covered by 5+ plans (potential redundancy). Makes traceability gaps visible before milestone completion.

items.title: Slack Standup Digest
items.description: Push a daily standup summary to a Slack channel: what completed yesterday, what's in-flight today, current blockers. Research teams using async collaboration need status visibility without manual updates. Integrates with existing STATE.md and roadmap data — no new data entry required from team members.

items.title: Hypothesis Tracking System
items.description: A structured way to record research hypotheses with their status (proposed, testing, confirmed, rejected) and link them to phases and eval results. Currently hypotheses live in freeform research docs and get lost. This makes scientific rigor explicit: you state what you expect, test it, and record whether it held — creating an audit trail of scientific reasoning.

items.title: Code Experiment Sandbox
items.description: Spin up an isolated branch with a minimal harness for trying a specific technique before committing to a full phase. Think 'spike' formalized: creates a time-boxed sandbox phase with auto-cleanup after N days. Prevents experimental code from polluting main branches while giving researchers a structured place to validate ideas cheaply before planning full execution.

items.title: Eval Benchmark Harness Integration
items.description: Native connectors for running standard ML benchmarks (MMLU, HumanEval, BIG-Bench, GLUE) as GRD eval targets, with results automatically written into EVAL.md. Researchers currently have to manually run harnesses and copy numbers. This closes the loop between standard benchmarks and GRD's eval tracking, making reproducing published results a one-command operation.

items.title: Phase Kickoff Readiness Checklist
items.description: Before executing a phase, verify a pre-flight checklist: baseline metrics captured, dependencies completed, research questions answered, compute resources available, eval plan written. Catches phases that are structurally ready (plans exist) but scientifically unready (no baseline to compare against). Prevents executing phases that will fail verification for avoidable reasons.

items.title: Milestone Retrospective Generator
items.description: After completing a milestone, synthesize a structured retrospective from all SUMMARY.md, REVIEW.md, and eval data: what worked, what didn't, how estimates compared to actuals, which techniques were abandoned and why. Captures institutional knowledge that otherwise evaporates at milestone boundaries, making future planning substantially more accurate.

items.title: Config Change Diff Viewer
items.description: Show a human-readable diff of config.json changes between sessions, explaining what each changed setting means for agent behavior. Users change settings like ceremony level, yolo mode, or execution concurrency without remembering their previous values or understanding downstream effects. This makes config drift visible and auditable.

items.title: Parallel Ablation Runner
items.description: Define an ablation matrix (e.g., learning rate × batch size × architecture) and automatically spawn parallel phases to test each combination, then collect results into a comparison table. Ablation studies are the most tedious part of ML research — this turns a week of manual experiment management into a declarative config plus one command.

items.title: Paper-to-Phase Converter
items.description: Given a paper slug from PAPERS.md, generate a draft implementation phase with plan structure, verification criteria, and eval metrics derived from the paper's methodology and reported results. Eliminates the gap between 'we want to implement this paper' and 'we have a concrete plan' — currently requires substantial manual translation work.

items.title: Decision Log Timeline
items.description: A chronological view of all decisions recorded across STATE.md, CONTEXT.md files, and discussion artifacts, with links to the phases they affected. Research projects accumulate hundreds of decisions that become hard to reconstruct later ('why did we choose X over Y?'). This surfaces the decision history without requiring users to read every planning file.

items.title: Live Eval Result Streaming
items.description: Stream eval metrics as they compute (per-sample or per-batch) into a live-updating terminal view, rather than waiting for the full eval to complete before seeing any results. Long evals (hours) give zero feedback until completion — this lets researchers abort early if results are clearly off-track, saving substantial compute time.

items.title: Cross-Artifact Full-Text Search
items.description: Search across all .planning/ artifacts — LANDSCAPE.md, PAPERS.md, KNOWHOW.md, SUMMARY.md, phase plans, decisions — with ranked results and context snippets. As projects grow to 50+ phases, finding where a specific technique or decision was discussed becomes a multi-minute grep session. This makes institutional knowledge instantly retrievable.

items.title: Agent Performance Analytics
items.description: Track which agents (executor, planner, reviewer, etc.) consume the most tokens, take the longest, and produce the most rework (measured by re-runs and failed verifications). Helps teams optimize ceremony levels and model profiles by showing actual cost-per-phase data rather than relying on intuition about which agents are expensive.

items.title: Phase Handoff Briefing
items.description: When starting a new work session mid-project, generate a focused briefing: what phase is active, what's completed vs. outstanding in the current phase, key decisions made, any blockers, and the single most important next action. Eliminates the 10-minute context reload that starts every session — teams can get to productive work in under a minute.

items.title: What-If Scenario Planner
items.description: Model the impact of scope changes, phase insertions, or requirement additions on milestone timeline and effort. When a stakeholder asks 'what if we add X?', the PM currently has to manually reason about cascading effects. This answers that question quantitatively using existing duration estimates and dependency graphs.

items.title: Semantic Todo Duplicate Detector
items.description: Before adding a new todo or phase, automatically check for semantically similar existing items (not just keyword overlap) and surface near-duplicates with similarity scores. The todo backlog already has 857 items with significant overlap — this prevents wasted planning effort and keeps the backlog actionable rather than a growing graveyard of redundant ideas.

items.title: Milestone Health Score
items.description: A composite score (0-100) computed from: phase completion rate, eval target hit rate, blocker age, estimate accuracy, and code review severity trends. Gives leadership a single number that reflects whether a milestone is on track, at risk, or in trouble — without requiring them to read detailed planning artifacts.

items.title: Research Reading List Curator
items.description: Based on the current milestone's LANDSCAPE.md and open research questions, automatically suggest 5-10 papers to read next, ranked by relevance and novelty relative to already-read papers. Researchers waste time deciding what to read next — this turns the survey knowledge base into an active recommendation engine that surfaces the highest-signal papers first.

items.title: Phase Retrospective Generator
items.description: After a phase completes, automatically generate a structured retrospective document from SUMMARY.md files, eval results, and git history. Surfaces what worked, what failed, and lessons learned. Solves the problem of institutional knowledge evaporating between milestones — future phases can reference past retrospectives to avoid repeating mistakes.

items.title: Research → Requirements Auto-Bridge
items.description: After a survey or deep-dive, automatically propose new REQUIREMENTS.md entries derived from the research findings. The agent identifies gaps between current requirements and what the literature suggests is needed. Eliminates the manual step of translating research insights into traceable requirements.

items.title: Phase Risk Scorer
items.description: Before executing a phase, score it across dimensions: scope clarity, dependency depth, novelty (how different from past phases), eval coverage. Output a risk dashboard that helps users decide whether to add more research, split the phase, or adjust the verification level. Prevents over-confident execution of poorly scoped phases.

items.title: Eval Regression Detector
items.description: Track eval metrics across phase executions and automatically flag regressions between iterations. If PSNR drops 2% or accuracy falls below baseline after a code change, trigger an alert before the phase is marked complete. Prevents silent regressions from shipping undetected across long iteration cycles.

items.title: Natural Language Roadmap Editor
items.description: Let users say 'move phase 4 after phase 6 and rename it to X' or 'split phase 3 into two phases' and have GRD apply the structural change to ROADMAP.md, renumber phases, and update all dependent references. Eliminates friction of manual YAML/markdown editing for roadmap restructuring.

items.title: Phase Dependency Graph Visualizer
items.description: Generate an SVG or Mermaid diagram of the phase dependency graph from ROADMAP.md, showing which phases are parallelizable, which are on the critical path, and current completion status. Helps project leads identify scheduling bottlenecks and opportunities to parallelize work.

items.title: Paper Citation Network Explorer
items.description: From PAPERS.md references, fetch citation graphs and surface which papers are most cited by the papers in your research base. Identify foundational works the team hasn't read yet. Solves the problem of missing seminal papers that could invalidate or strengthen an approach.

items.title: Milestone Velocity Forecast
items.description: Using historical phase duration data from SUMMARY.md metrics, forecast remaining milestone completion date with confidence intervals. Shows velocity trend (accelerating/decelerating) and flags phases that are likely to slip based on past patterns. Helps teams set realistic expectations with stakeholders.

items.title: Research Hypothesis Tracker
items.description: Structured tracking of research hypotheses: state the hypothesis, link it to phases that test it, and automatically update its status (confirmed/refuted/inconclusive) from eval results. Prevents the common problem where teams lose track of why they started a line of inquiry and whether it was validated.

items.title: Interactive Eval Results Dashboard
items.description: Generate an HTML dashboard from all EVAL.md and SUMMARY.md files showing metric trends over time with charts. Exportable as a self-contained HTML file. Solves the pain of manually scanning dozens of markdown files to understand whether the project is improving or stagnating.

items.title: Plan Complexity Estimator
items.description: Before plan execution, analyze the PLAN.md to estimate complexity: count must_haves, count files touched, identify cross-module dependencies, detect novelty vs. prior work. Output an effort estimate with reasoning. Helps users decide whether to split plans or add buffer time before execution.

items.title: Research Gap Spotter
items.description: Compare current LANDSCAPE.md against the phase's stated success criteria and identify research questions that aren't answered by any known paper or prior experiment. Surface these as explicit unknowns that need addressing before execution. Prevents executing plans built on shaky research foundations.

items.title: Daily Standup Report Generator
items.description: Generate a concise daily standup report from STATE.md, recent SUMMARY.md files, and git log: what was completed yesterday, what's in progress today, and any blockers. Output as plain text or post directly to Slack. Eliminates the manual overhead of writing status updates for async teams.

items.title: Phase Split Advisor
items.description: Detect when a phase has grown too large (too many plans, too broad a goal, too many file changes) and recommend how to split it into two focused phases with clear boundaries. Solves the common anti-pattern of 'mega-phases' that are hard to verify and take too long to execute.

items.title: Requirements Coverage Heatmap
items.description: For each requirement in REQUIREMENTS.md, show which phases address it, whether it has tests, and whether it's been verified. Render as a color-coded matrix. Ensures no requirement falls through the cracks between phases and makes audit trails visible.

items.title: Experiment Reproducibility Checker
items.description: Scan SUMMARY.md files for experiment results and verify that the code, data, and config needed to reproduce them are still present and unchanged (using checksums). Alert when experiments become non-reproducible due to code drift. Critical for research credibility.

items.title: PR → Phase Auto-Mapper
items.description: When a PR is opened, automatically detect which GRD phase it corresponds to (by branch name, commit messages, or file patterns), attach the PR link to the phase SUMMARY.md, and update tracker status. Closes the loop between code review and R&D tracking without manual effort.

items.title: Context Window Optimizer
items.description: Before agent invocations, analyze which planning artifacts are actually needed and prune irrelevant content. For a phase-5 execution, don't load phase-1 research. Reduces token costs and improves agent focus. Surfaces estimated token savings to the user.

items.title: Agent Cost Tracker
items.description: Track estimated token usage and cost per phase, per agent, and per milestone. Show cost-to-date and cost forecast in the progress dashboard. Helps teams budget API usage and identify unexpectedly expensive phases before they run over budget.

items.title: One-Click Milestone Report
items.description: Generate a polished PDF or HTML milestone completion report: executive summary, phases completed, key metrics, eval results, lessons learned, and next steps. Suitable for sharing with stakeholders who don't use GRD. Eliminates hours of manual report writing at milestone close.

items.title: Eval Target Negotiator
items.description: When success criteria are too ambitious or trivially easy based on baseline metrics, suggest calibrated targets with rationale. If baseline PSNR is 28dB and the target is 40dB, flag this as unrealistic and suggest 30dB as a stretch goal. Prevents wasted iteration cycles chasing unachievable targets.

items.title: Method Ablation Planner
items.description: From a PLAN.md describing a multi-component method, automatically generate an ablation study plan that isolates each component's contribution. Produces a structured EVAL.md section with ablation experiment designs. Ensures research claims are properly supported by evidence.

items.title: Blocker Escalation Workflow
items.description: When a blocker is added to STATE.md and remains unresolved for >24 hours, automatically draft an escalation message with context, options, and recommended next steps. Optionally post to Slack or GitHub. Prevents blockers from silently stalling projects for days.

items.title: Phase Template Library
items.description: Curated PLAN.md templates for common R&D phase types: 'baseline evaluation', 'architecture comparison', 'hyperparameter search', 'data pipeline', 'inference optimization'. Users pick a template and get a pre-structured plan with appropriate verification levels and common must_haves. Reduces planning time from hours to minutes.

items.title: Git Blame → Phase Tracer
items.description: Given a file and line range, trace back to the GRD phase that introduced that code. Links git commits to phase SUMMARY.md files via branch naming conventions. Answers the question 'why was this written this way?' by surfacing the original plan and research rationale.

items.title: Multi-Repo Project Federation
items.description: Aggregate progress, blockers, and eval results from multiple GRD-managed repositories into a single federated dashboard. For research teams where different components live in separate repos. Solves the visibility problem for technical leads managing distributed R&D efforts.

items.title: What-If Phase Simulator
items.description: Before committing to a phase plan, simulate alternative approaches: 'what if we use method A vs method B?' and generate skeleton plans for each approach with estimated tradeoffs in complexity, risk, and expected eval gain. Helps teams make informed architecture decisions before writing code.

items.title: Smart Phase Ordering Recommender
items.description: Analyze the dependency graph and phase properties (risk, effort, uncertainty) and suggest an optimized execution order that front-loads risk reduction and enables maximum parallelism. Goes beyond simple dependency resolution to optimize for project success probability.

items.title: Live Execution Log Stream
items.description: Stream agent execution logs in real time to a terminal UI with color coding by agent type, elapsed time, and current operation. Users can see exactly what the executor agent is doing without waiting for the final SUMMARY.md. Reduces anxiety during long autonomous execution runs.

items.title: Paper Implementation Validator
items.description: Given a research paper and its code implementation in the project, validate that key algorithm steps from the paper are reflected in the code (via semantic comparison of paper pseudocode to function names and logic). Catches implementation drift from the intended algorithm early.

items.title: Milestone Changelog Generator
items.description: Auto-generate a user-facing CHANGELOG.md entry from all phase SUMMARY.md files when a milestone is completed. Summarizes features added, metrics improved, bugs fixed, and breaking changes. Eliminates the tedious work of writing changelogs manually from dozens of technical summaries.

items.title: Interactive New Phase Wizard
items.description: A guided TUI wizard for adding a new phase: asks about goal, type, dependencies, duration, and success criteria with smart defaults and validation at each step. Much friendlier than hand-editing ROADMAP.md. Reduces onboarding friction for users new to GRD's planning model.

items.title: Research Freshness Monitor
items.description: Track the age of papers and survey data in LANDSCAPE.md and alert when key references are >12 months old in a fast-moving field. Suggest running a fresh survey to update the landscape. Prevents teams from executing plans based on outdated SoTA that has since been superseded.

items.description: error() in lib/scaffold.ts at line 167 says "template type required: summary, plan, or verification. Usag..." but does not suggest what the user should do to fix it. Add an actionable recovery hint.

items.title: Add recovery hint to error in tracker.ts line 1075
items.description: error() in lib/tracker.ts at line 1075 says "Usage: tracker update-status <phase-number> <status>. Provid..." but does not suggest what the user should do to fix it. Add an actionable recovery hint.

items.title: Add recovery hint to error in tracker.ts line 1386
items.description: error() in lib/tracker.ts at line 1386 says "Usage: tracker prepare-phase-sync <phase-number>. Provide th..." but does not suggest what the user should do to fix it. Add an actionable recovery hint.

items.title: Add recovery hint to error in worktree.ts line 436
items.description: error() in lib/worktree.ts at line 436 says "Either --phase or --path is required for worktree remove. Sp..." but does not suggest what the user should do to fix it. Add an actionable recovery hint.

Researchers often start new projects in adjacent domains where prior survey work is highly relevant. Instead of re-running expensive surveys, they can pull vetted knowledge from a sibling project and annotate what transfers vs. what needs updating.

Users currently have to manually diff eval results — this makes regressions impossible to miss and catches them before they compound across phases.

items.title: Plan Template Library
items.description: A curated set of PLAN.md templates for common R&D patterns: ablation study, dataset collection, model fine-tuning, API integration, benchmark reproduction. When running /grd:plan-phase, the planner can select or suggest a template as a starting scaffold. Reduces the blank-page problem and ensures common verification steps aren't forgotten.

items.description: Ask questions like 'what did we ship last week?', 'which phases are over budget?', 'what blockers are unresolved?' and get structured answers synthesized from STATE.md, SUMMARY.md files, and roadmap. Currently users must manually read multiple files. This makes project status instantly accessible without context-loading overhead.

items.title: Experiment Notebook Export
items.description: Export a phase's research + plans + summaries + eval results as a self-contained Jupyter notebook or Markdown report suitable for sharing with stakeholders or publishing as a blog post. Bridges the gap between internal R&D artifacts and external communication — currently researchers must manually reformat everything when presenting results.

items.title: Automated Literature Gap Detection
items.description: After surveying a topic, automatically compare the methods in LANDSCAPE.md against the implementation in plans/code to identify research areas that were surveyed but never attempted. Surfaces 'we read about X but never tried it' gaps, helping teams decide whether to revisit promising approaches before closing a milestone.

items.title: Phase Time Budget Tracking
items.description: Track actual wall-clock time per phase against the declared Duration field, and surface variance trends across the project. Teams consistently underestimate phase duration — this gives historical data to calibrate future estimates and flags phases that are running 2x+ over budget before they derail the milestone timeline.

items.title: Multi-Repo GRD Federation
items.description: A parent project that aggregates STATE.md and progress from multiple child GRD projects (e.g., separate repos for data pipeline, model training, and serving). Research platforms often span multiple repos — this gives a unified dashboard showing cross-repo phase status, blockers, and eval metrics in one view without requiring a monorepo.

items.title: Requirement Coverage Heatmap
items.description: Visualize which requirements from REQUIREMENTS.md are covered by which phases and plans, with color coding for coverage density. Uncovers requirements that have zero plan coverage (at risk of being forgotten) and requirements covered by 5+ plans (potential redundancy). Makes traceability gaps visible before milestone completion.

items.title: Slack Standup Digest
items.description: Push a daily standup summary to a Slack channel: what completed yesterday, what's in-flight today, current blockers. Research teams using async collaboration need status visibility without manual updates. Integrates with existing STATE.md and roadmap data — no new data entry required from team members.

items.title: Hypothesis Tracking System
items.description: A structured way to record research hypotheses with their status (proposed, testing, confirmed, rejected) and link them to phases and eval results. Currently hypotheses live in freeform research docs and get lost. This makes scientific rigor explicit: you state what you expect, test it, and record whether it held — creating an audit trail of scientific reasoning.

items.title: Code Experiment Sandbox
items.description: Spin up an isolated branch with a minimal harness for trying a specific technique before committing to a full phase. Think 'spike' formalized: creates a time-boxed sandbox phase with auto-cleanup after N days. Prevents experimental code from polluting main branches while giving researchers a structured place to validate ideas cheaply before planning full execution.

items.title: Eval Benchmark Harness Integration
items.description: Native connectors for running standard ML benchmarks (MMLU, HumanEval, BIG-Bench, GLUE) as GRD eval targets, with results automatically written into EVAL.md. Researchers currently have to manually run harnesses and copy numbers. This closes the loop between standard benchmarks and GRD's eval tracking, making reproducing published results a one-command operation.

items.title: Phase Kickoff Readiness Checklist
items.description: Before executing a phase, verify a pre-flight checklist: baseline metrics captured, dependencies completed, research questions answered, compute resources available, eval plan written. Catches phases that are structurally ready (plans exist) but scientifically unready (no baseline to compare against). Prevents executing phases that will fail verification for avoidable reasons.

items.title: Milestone Retrospective Generator
items.description: After completing a milestone, synthesize a structured retrospective from all SUMMARY.md, REVIEW.md, and eval data: what worked, what didn't, how estimates compared to actuals, which techniques were abandoned and why. Captures institutional knowledge that otherwise evaporates at milestone boundaries, making future planning substantially more accurate.

items.title: Config Change Diff Viewer
items.description: Show a human-readable diff of config.json changes between sessions, explaining what each changed setting means for agent behavior. Users change settings like ceremony level, yolo mode, or execution concurrency without remembering their previous values or understanding downstream effects. This makes config drift visible and auditable.

items.title: Parallel Ablation Runner
items.description: Define an ablation matrix (e.g., learning rate × batch size × architecture) and automatically spawn parallel phases to test each combination, then collect results into a comparison table. Ablation studies are the most tedious part of ML research — this turns a week of manual experiment management into a declarative config plus one command.

items.title: Paper-to-Phase Converter
items.description: Given a paper slug from PAPERS.md, generate a draft implementation phase with plan structure, verification criteria, and eval metrics derived from the paper's methodology and reported results. Eliminates the gap between 'we want to implement this paper' and 'we have a concrete plan' — currently requires substantial manual translation work.

items.title: Decision Log Timeline
items.description: A chronological view of all decisions recorded across STATE.md, CONTEXT.md files, and discussion artifacts, with links to the phases they affected. Research projects accumulate hundreds of decisions that become hard to reconstruct later ('why did we choose X over Y?'). This surfaces the decision history without requiring users to read every planning file.

items.title: Live Eval Result Streaming
items.description: Stream eval metrics as they compute (per-sample or per-batch) into a live-updating terminal view, rather than waiting for the full eval to complete before seeing any results. Long evals (hours) give zero feedback until completion — this lets researchers abort early if results are clearly off-track, saving substantial compute time.

items.title: Cross-Artifact Full-Text Search
items.description: Search across all .planning/ artifacts — LANDSCAPE.md, PAPERS.md, KNOWHOW.md, SUMMARY.md, phase plans, decisions — with ranked results and context snippets. As projects grow to 50+ phases, finding where a specific technique or decision was discussed becomes a multi-minute grep session. This makes institutional knowledge instantly retrievable.

items.title: Agent Performance Analytics
items.description: Track which agents (executor, planner, reviewer, etc.) consume the most tokens, take the longest, and produce the most rework (measured by re-runs and failed verifications). Helps teams optimize ceremony levels and model profiles by showing actual cost-per-phase data rather than relying on intuition about which agents are expensive.

items.title: Phase Handoff Briefing
items.description: When starting a new work session mid-project, generate a focused briefing: what phase is active, what's completed vs. outstanding in the current phase, key decisions made, any blockers, and the single most important next action. Eliminates the 10-minute context reload that starts every session — teams can get to productive work in under a minute.

items.title: What-If Scenario Planner
items.description: Model the impact of scope changes, phase insertions, or requirement additions on milestone timeline and effort. When a stakeholder asks 'what if we add X?', the PM currently has to manually reason about cascading effects. This answers that question quantitatively using existing duration estimates and dependency graphs.

items.title: Semantic Todo Duplicate Detector
items.description: Before adding a new todo or phase, automatically check for semantically similar existing items (not just keyword overlap) and surface near-duplicates with similarity scores. The todo backlog already has 857 items with significant overlap — this prevents wasted planning effort and keeps the backlog actionable rather than a growing graveyard of redundant ideas.

items.title: Milestone Health Score
items.description: A composite score (0-100) computed from: phase completion rate, eval target hit rate, blocker age, estimate accuracy, and code review severity trends. Gives leadership a single number that reflects whether a milestone is on track, at risk, or in trouble — without requiring them to read detailed planning artifacts.

items.title: Research Reading List Curator
items.description: Based on the current milestone's LANDSCAPE.md and open research questions, automatically suggest 5-10 papers to read next, ranked by relevance and novelty relative to already-read papers. Researchers waste time deciding what to read next — this turns the survey knowledge base into an active recommendation engine that surfaces the highest-signal papers first.

items.title: Phase Retrospective Generator
items.description: After a phase completes, automatically generate a structured retrospective document from SUMMARY.md files, eval results, and git history. Surfaces what worked, what failed, and lessons learned. Solves the problem of institutional knowledge evaporating between milestones — future phases can reference past retrospectives to avoid repeating mistakes.

items.title: Research → Requirements Auto-Bridge
items.description: After a survey or deep-dive, automatically propose new REQUIREMENTS.md entries derived from the research findings. The agent identifies gaps between current requirements and what the literature suggests is needed. Eliminates the manual step of translating research insights into traceable requirements.

items.title: Phase Risk Scorer
items.description: Before executing a phase, score it across dimensions: scope clarity, dependency depth, novelty (how different from past phases), eval coverage. Output a risk dashboard that helps users decide whether to add more research, split the phase, or adjust the verification level. Prevents over-confident execution of poorly scoped phases.

items.title: Eval Regression Detector
items.description: Track eval metrics across phase executions and automatically flag regressions between iterations. If PSNR drops 2% or accuracy falls below baseline after a code change, trigger an alert before the phase is marked complete. Prevents silent regressions from shipping undetected across long iteration cycles.

items.title: Natural Language Roadmap Editor
items.description: Let users say 'move phase 4 after phase 6 and rename it to X' or 'split phase 3 into two phases' and have GRD apply the structural change to ROADMAP.md, renumber phases, and update all dependent references. Eliminates friction of manual YAML/markdown editing for roadmap restructuring.

items.title: Phase Dependency Graph Visualizer
items.description: Generate an SVG or Mermaid diagram of the phase dependency graph from ROADMAP.md, showing which phases are parallelizable, which are on the critical path, and current completion status. Helps project leads identify scheduling bottlenecks and opportunities to parallelize work.

items.title: Paper Citation Network Explorer
items.description: From PAPERS.md references, fetch citation graphs and surface which papers are most cited by the papers in your research base. Identify foundational works the team hasn't read yet. Solves the problem of missing seminal papers that could invalidate or strengthen an approach.

items.title: Milestone Velocity Forecast
items.description: Using historical phase duration data from SUMMARY.md metrics, forecast remaining milestone completion date with confidence intervals. Shows velocity trend (accelerating/decelerating) and flags phases that are likely to slip based on past patterns. Helps teams set realistic expectations with stakeholders.

items.title: Research Hypothesis Tracker
items.description: Structured tracking of research hypotheses: state the hypothesis, link it to phases that test it, and automatically update its status (confirmed/refuted/inconclusive) from eval results. Prevents the common problem where teams lose track of why they started a line of inquiry and whether it was validated.

items.title: Interactive Eval Results Dashboard
items.description: Generate an HTML dashboard from all EVAL.md and SUMMARY.md files showing metric trends over time with charts. Exportable as a self-contained HTML file. Solves the pain of manually scanning dozens of markdown files to understand whether the project is improving or stagnating.

items.title: Plan Complexity Estimator
items.description: Before plan execution, analyze the PLAN.md to estimate complexity: count must_haves, count files touched, identify cross-module dependencies, detect novelty vs. prior work. Output an effort estimate with reasoning. Helps users decide whether to split plans or add buffer time before execution.

items.title: Research Gap Spotter
items.description: Compare current LANDSCAPE.md against the phase's stated success criteria and identify research questions that aren't answered by any known paper or prior experiment. Surface these as explicit unknowns that need addressing before execution. Prevents executing plans built on shaky research foundations.

items.title: Daily Standup Report Generator
items.description: Generate a concise daily standup report from STATE.md, recent SUMMARY.md files, and git log: what was completed yesterday, what's in progress today, and any blockers. Output as plain text or post directly to Slack. Eliminates the manual overhead of writing status updates for async teams.

items.title: Phase Split Advisor
items.description: Detect when a phase has grown too large (too many plans, too broad a goal, too many file changes) and recommend how to split it into two focused phases with clear boundaries. Solves the common anti-pattern of 'mega-phases' that are hard to verify and take too long to execute.

items.title: Requirements Coverage Heatmap
items.description: For each requirement in REQUIREMENTS.md, show which phases address it, whether it has tests, and whether it's been verified. Render as a color-coded matrix. Ensures no requirement falls through the cracks between phases and makes audit trails visible.

items.title: Experiment Reproducibility Checker
items.description: Scan SUMMARY.md files for experiment results and verify that the code, data, and config needed to reproduce them are still present and unchanged (using checksums). Alert when experiments become non-reproducible due to code drift. Critical for research credibility.

items.title: PR → Phase Auto-Mapper
items.description: When a PR is opened, automatically detect which GRD phase it corresponds to (by branch name, commit messages, or file patterns), attach the PR link to the phase SUMMARY.md, and update tracker status. Closes the loop between code review and R&D tracking without manual effort.

items.title: Context Window Optimizer
items.description: Before agent invocations, analyze which planning artifacts are actually needed and prune irrelevant content. For a phase-5 execution, don't load phase-1 research. Reduces token costs and improves agent focus. Surfaces estimated token savings to the user.

items.title: Agent Cost Tracker
items.description: Track estimated token usage and cost per phase, per agent, and per milestone. Show cost-to-date and cost forecast in the progress dashboard. Helps teams budget API usage and identify unexpectedly expensive phases before they run over budget.

items.title: One-Click Milestone Report
items.description: Generate a polished PDF or HTML milestone completion report: executive summary, phases completed, key metrics, eval results, lessons learned, and next steps. Suitable for sharing with stakeholders who don't use GRD. Eliminates hours of manual report writing at milestone close.

items.title: Eval Target Negotiator
items.description: When success criteria are too ambitious or trivially easy based on baseline metrics, suggest calibrated targets with rationale. If baseline PSNR is 28dB and the target is 40dB, flag this as unrealistic and suggest 30dB as a stretch goal. Prevents wasted iteration cycles chasing unachievable targets.

items.title: Method Ablation Planner
items.description: From a PLAN.md describing a multi-component method, automatically generate an ablation study plan that isolates each component's contribution. Produces a structured EVAL.md section with ablation experiment designs. Ensures research claims are properly supported by evidence.

items.title: Blocker Escalation Workflow
items.description: When a blocker is added to STATE.md and remains unresolved for >24 hours, automatically draft an escalation message with context, options, and recommended next steps. Optionally post to Slack or GitHub. Prevents blockers from silently stalling projects for days.

items.title: Phase Template Library
items.description: Curated PLAN.md templates for common R&D phase types: 'baseline evaluation', 'architecture comparison', 'hyperparameter search', 'data pipeline', 'inference optimization'. Users pick a template and get a pre-structured plan with appropriate verification levels and common must_haves. Reduces planning time from hours to minutes.

items.title: Git Blame → Phase Tracer
items.description: Given a file and line range, trace back to the GRD phase that introduced that code. Links git commits to phase SUMMARY.md files via branch naming conventions. Answers the question 'why was this written this way?' by surfacing the original plan and research rationale.

items.title: Multi-Repo Project Federation
items.description: Aggregate progress, blockers, and eval results from multiple GRD-managed repositories into a single federated dashboard. For research teams where different components live in separate repos. Solves the visibility problem for technical leads managing distributed R&D efforts.

items.title: What-If Phase Simulator
items.description: Before committing to a phase plan, simulate alternative approaches: 'what if we use method A vs method B?' and generate skeleton plans for each approach with estimated tradeoffs in complexity, risk, and expected eval gain. Helps teams make informed architecture decisions before writing code.

items.title: Smart Phase Ordering Recommender
items.description: Analyze the dependency graph and phase properties (risk, effort, uncertainty) and suggest an optimized execution order that front-loads risk reduction and enables maximum parallelism. Goes beyond simple dependency resolution to optimize for project success probability.

items.title: Live Execution Log Stream
items.description: Stream agent execution logs in real time to a terminal UI with color coding by agent type, elapsed time, and current operation. Users can see exactly what the executor agent is doing without waiting for the final SUMMARY.md. Reduces anxiety during long autonomous execution runs.

items.title: Paper Implementation Validator
items.description: Given a research paper and its code implementation in the project, validate that key algorithm steps from the paper are reflected in the code (via semantic comparison of paper pseudocode to function names and logic). Catches implementation drift from the intended algorithm early.

items.title: Milestone Changelog Generator
items.description: Auto-generate a user-facing CHANGELOG.md entry from all phase SUMMARY.md files when a milestone is completed. Summarizes features added, metrics improved, bugs fixed, and breaking changes. Eliminates the tedious work of writing changelogs manually from dozens of technical summaries.

items.title: Interactive New Phase Wizard
items.description: A guided TUI wizard for adding a new phase: asks about goal, type, dependencies, duration, and success criteria with smart defaults and validation at each step. Much friendlier than hand-editing ROADMAP.md. Reduces onboarding friction for users new to GRD's planning model.

items.title: Research Freshness Monitor
items.description: Track the age of papers and survey data in LANDSCAPE.md and alert when key references are >12 months old in a fast-moving field. Suggest running a fresh survey to update the landscape. Prevents teams from executing plans based on outdated SoTA that has since been superseded.
